Content:
Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:


For Local and Foreign Entity

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Account Activation

1

Merchant sign up for Xendit on their own via the local dashboard

Merchant

MO Process Application Request

2

2a. Once merchant has done online submission, the application reflects in #malaysia-pg-activation-request slack channel and/or Admin Dashboard (AD) under Onboarding > Account Management.

Application Submission Example :
A. Slack Channel (#malaysia-pg-activation-request )

B: Dashboard

MO

3

MO will process Account Activation request from Admin Dashboard > Onboarding Management > Select Malaysia > Tab “Merchant Ops”

Merchants with the following statuses are ready for review.

  • Pending Verification

  • Verification in Progress

4

MO to select the merchant profile and tick on Start KYC button to proceed with KYC verification.

5

MO needs to ensure merchants select the correct entity type based on SSM business structure (sole prop/sdn bhd/bhd etc).

If the merchant has selected the wrong country incorporated / entity type. MO to proceed to create workflow requests to (Temp) Entity change request for approval and changes by product team.

6

Always double check against our high-risk industry listing.

7

IF Low & Medium Risk Merchant

Please proceed with the KYC for merchants based on their entity type and submitted documents.

LDD Verification: Verify legal documents based on Entity Type.
BDD Verification: Ensure the website/social media storefront is compliant with market-specific regulations.
Screening & ComplyAdvantage

  • Execute screening for all key stakeholders (UBOs, Directors, Signatories).

  • Review all hits for Sanctions, PEP, or Adverse Media.

  • If a potential "True Hit" is found, escalate to Compliance

8

IF HIGH AND VERY HIGH RISK:

Before activation, MO must check the AML Risk Rating. While the Dashboard auto-blocks High/Very High risk levels, MO is responsible for manually identifying prohibited triggers and initiating the escalation to Compliance.

1. Automated Risk Rating (Sub-industry Logic)

Check the system-assigned risk score based on the merchant's industry:

  • Rating 0 (Standard): No escalation needed. Proceed with standard KYC.

  • Rating 3 (High Risk): STOP. High-risk industry detected. Escalate to Compliance.

2. Prohibited Risk Indicators (Manual Red Flags)

Immediately Escalate to Compliance if any of the following "Prohibited" triggers are found during the document or website review:

  • Entity incorporation, or any Stakeholder (UBO, Director, Signatory) nationality/address is linked to a Prohibited Country.

  • The merchant operates within a Prohibited Industry or an unauthorized high-risk sector.

  • Merchants from high-risk industries. (ie: PSP and Reseller model for XP sub merchant, Foreign Entities, direct selling/MLM)

9

When a merchant is escalated due to high AML risk or onboarding triggers, the following governance applies:

  • EDD is conducted by the Country Compliance Team of the market where activation is requested.

  • Final approval must be obtained from the respective Country Office(s); Country Compliance (L2) is responsible for securing this approval.

  • MO Agents must ensure all EDD documentation and approval notes are clearly logged in the Admin Dashboard to maintain a valid Audit Trail.

Sample dashboard :

MO/Compliance Team/Country Compliance (L2)

KYC Support & Escalation Actions

While performing KYC checks, MO Agents can use the available action buttons in the Admin Dashboard to escalate cases, request support, or flag merchants that require special attention. These actions help streamline cross-team collaboration and ensure that merchants are reviewed accurately and efficiently.

Below are the available actions and their respective purposes:

  1. Need MO prioritization: Agent on shift needs to prioritize this merchant

  2. Need Sales action: Agents can click the Flag Button to get more information or clarification from Merchant. Sales team will get notified on #onboarding-notif channel whenever this button is clicked.

  3. Need MR advice: Agents can click the Warning Sign Button to get advice from the Merchant-Risk Team, usually regarding the merchant's website. Mrisk team will get notified on #merchant-risk channel whenever this button is clicked.

Account Status and KYC Status

During merchant onboarding, the Admin Dashboard reflects two key indicators: Account Status and KYC Status. Together, these statuses provide visibility into the progress and outcome of the KYC process and guide MO Agents on how to proceed with each merchant case.

Use the table below as a reference to interpret each status combination and determine the appropriate next steps.

Account Status

KYC Status

Meaning

Next Step

AWAITING_DOCS

AWAITING_DATA

Merchant hasn’t completed all requirement for Account Activation

Merchant will need to complete required document/information

AWAITING_DOCS

INVALID

Agents have assessed merchant's application, but there’s document/info that is still missing/ required from merchant

Merchant will need to come back with the requested document/ information

PENDING_VERIFICATION

DATA_PROVIDED

Merchant already submitted the requirements, waiting to be KYC-ed

MO agents need to do KYC for this merchant

PENDING_VERIFICATION

PENDING

Merchant already submitted the requirements, but agents need more clarification from Xendit’s internal team (e.g: Sales/MRisk/ Compliance)

MO will need to follow up until case is solved and inform Sales/AM/Merchant for any additional requirements

DECLINED

FAILED

Merchant not pass KYC & can’t onboard with Xendit due to ineligibility in their application

LIVE

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC and has now onboarded with Xendit

Proceed to do channel activation (by request from merchant)

AWAITING_SIGN

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC but hasn’t signed Service Agreement

Info AM/Sales to notify merchant for signing Service Agreement in order to “Go-Live” (onboard with Xendit)

SUSPENDED

PASSED

Account already LIVE, but upon further investigation it is found that merchant is fraudulent, so their account is freeze / got suspended

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.

  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit

  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit

  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Additionally, merchants operating in Malaysia must adhere to additional standards to ensure legal transparency. Their website/app must include:

  • Terms & Conditions, Privacy Policy, and Refund Policy.

  • Contact information (email, telephone number, address to follow the business address as per SSM)

  • SSM company registered number and registered name to verify ownership

  • Social Media (Facebook/Instagram) profiles must display the Registered Company Name, Registration Number, and Contact Details ( number, company address and email address) in the profile info.

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

1

MO needs to KYC and review the following company details:

A. SSM & Entity Validation:

  • Cross-reference submitted data with the SSM Profile. Ensure the Entity Type (Sole Prop/Sdn Bhd/Bhd) matches exactly.
    - If the country or entity type is wrong, trigger a (Temp) Entity change request to the Product Team for approval and changes.

B. Website/Social Media Audit

- Ensure the website/social media/shopfront matches with merchant sub category selection.

Sample on what to take note dashboard :

- Check whether the website meets the general and additional website/social media criteria.

MO

2

Tick on if the specific document/field is correct and valid.

Tick if the information is incorrect, blurry, or invalid.

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Dilligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not (bank book scan is not needed to be checked anymore not unless needed e.g. High Risk Industry and if requested by Compliance).

2

Additionally, MO needs to conduct Authorise Representative, Directorship & Shareholding due diligence.

An authorised representative must have a letter of authorisation duly signed by the director if the person is not one of the shareholder/director of the company.

3

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

Legal Documents Required for MY

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Malaysia NRIC (MyKad) / National ID

  • Malaysia NRIC (MyKad) / National ID

  • Front and back

Passport

  • For both Malaysian and non-malaysian

    • Should not be expired

    • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

Proof of Business

- Photo of the store/offline store

- Invoice / Purchase Order (PO)

Business Registration Documents

For Sabah:

- Lesen Berniaga issued by DBKK


For Sarawak:

- Sijil Pendaftaran (Ordinan Nama-Nama Perniagaan Bab 64)


- Ordinan Perniagaan, Profesion dan Perlesenan Perdagangan Borang 1

- Pengesahan Pendaftaran Nama Perniagaan with details of proprietor or partner

- Cabutan nama perniagaan of the company containing all director or shareholder details

For Sabah:

  • Lesen Berniaga

  • Any business document that shows the particulars of directors or shareholders & Premise address

  • Should not be expired

For Sarawak:

  • Pengesahan Pendaftaran Nama Perniagaan- should have the details of proprietor or partner

  • Cabutan nama perniagaan of the company - should contain all director or shareholder details

  • Should not be expired

SSM Certificate of Incorporation (Form D or E)

SSM e-info / SSM Profile

  • Should show full page/pages

  • The document should be extracted within 3 months of onboarding.

Limited Liability Partnerships (LLP) Profile

  • includes all details of the company

  • Should show full page/pages

  • The document should be extracted within 3 months of onboarding.

Authorization Letter

  • Required only if the Authorised Signatory (the person signing with Xendit) is not a Director.

  • We accept a letter of authorization or board resolution appointing the Authorised Signatory if the person is not the Director, along with identification documents of the director signing the authorisation letter.

Merchant's proof of address
(only required if the place of business is different from the registered business address in the corporate document)

  • utility bill

  • phone bill

  • bank statement

  • tenancy agreement

  • tenancy agreement - must be under the business name and issued within three months of application

Certificate of incorporation of LLP

Latest organization chart

  • Should indicate full name, NRIC / Passport number

  • Required only for association Entity

Proof of Incorporation

  • Documents issued by the local regulators in the country where the entity was incorporated.

  • Required only for association Entity

Business licenses (only if necessary for certain high-risk industry)

  1. Sample of Medical Device license from MDA

  1. Sample of Liquor license

  1. Sample of MY E-hailing operator service

For more information, you may refer here: https://docs.xendit.co/docs/malaysia-business-documents

PEP (Politically Exposed Person)

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening is a critical component of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) regulation. It involves verifying a merchant’s identity to determine whether they, or their related parties, are classified as Politically Exposed Persons.

MO to perform due diligence in SSM profile to ensure all the directors / shareholders are under individual and UBO verified. All the director & shareholder needs to go through screening test via comply advantage if not listed as stakeholder during submission.

For foreign directors/shareholders, merchants must provide the passport copy & proof of residence to ensure merchants do not fall under prohibited or high risk countries.

You may refer here for more details:

Merchant Screening SOP

SOP for Handling Screening Hits

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why documents marked as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS

  • AML

  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE

Edge Cases

These are unusual, rare, or boundary situations that don’t happen often but can cause problems if the SOP doesn’t address them. They sit outside “normal operations.”

Local Entity

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

MO Process Merchant’s Request

1

Eg. Payex sub-own for OCBC cards activation only

1a. Once MO MY clicked to activate via Payex terminal, has done online submission, the application will reflect in #malaysia-pg-activation-request slack channel

Application Submission Example :

1b. To enter Xendit Dashboard, under Onboarding tab, in Account Management & search for the BID, will appear under XP Sub (Owned) - Payex, click on BID

1c. Under Merchant Ops Note: to edit & insert Payex sub & click save

1d. Click Pass & leave it as “awaiting sign”

1e. Back to the slack thread & put reaction Payex emoji

MY MO

2

Eg. Sub-managed merchants under referral



2a. To do KYC as usual


2b. If further information / documents required, to request merchant’s resubmission via self serve email link >> https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/17b3Z2Mj8M2llNEstNtqwvO_cY8M6UWu1r_ofTh5R8Z8/edit?gid=1620007028#gid=1620007028


2c. Merchant’s email reply will reflect in #ca-email-notification slack channel and will be tag to MY MO to monitor responses


2d. To do KYC upon resubmission & to approve once all are ok to proceed

Foreign Entity

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

MO Process Merchant’s Request

1

Eg. Enjoylink

1a. Once merchant has done online submission, the application will reflect in #malaysia-pg-activation-request slack channel
(can also identify via email address @ enjoy.link)

Application Submission Example :


1b. To do KYC as usual, tick on X if incorrect & to put in correct reason

1c. Specific NOB, eg. for gaming NOB, to download Gaming EDD Questionnaire >> https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kOqnyqFgDi3KR2GW3T_8nRuG8roXuwntGMUYy2nrOnk/edit?tab=t.0 & to add on Gaming EDD Questionnaire for merchant to fill up, can be under any empty parts example business license

1d. Change under Status, in KYC Status change to “awaiting resubmission” and inform in slack tag to OIM on all request so that merchant do own resubmission via dashboard

1e. Once received, to do another round KYC and approve if all good
Note: to escalate to MY Compliance to get approval for merchant’s filled up Gaming EDD Questionnaire, before going live for the merchant

Sales/OIM

Regional Merchant

Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Account Activation

1

Merchants register via the Xendit Registration Link (for regional accounts)

NOTE: Sole Proprietorships are not eligible for the Global/SG Dashboard.

Merchant

MO Process Application Request

2

MO monitors the #mo-request Slack channel. The assigned @mo-my-on-shift will be tagged and should perform the KYC.

Request example:

MO

3

MO will process Account Activation request from Admin Dashboard > Onboarding Management > Select Singapore > Tab “Merchant Ops” > use BID to search for the account

MO

4

The MO agent needs to validate the risk score of the merchant. If they are very high risk, do not proceed. Escalate immediately to the Risk Team via #risk-request for fraud-specific approval.

5

Otherwise, MO to select the merchant profile and click on Start KYC button to proceed with KYC verification.

6

Ensure Industry is not tagged as restricted

  • If restricted - need to click edit & choose more specific industry, ensure click on v2 related

7

Proceed with BDD and LDD review then click on “Save and Screen”

8

Look at (top) Screening Status: Decision Required (this means there are something needed further action in ComplyAdvantage)

Scroll down further, will appear beside each subsection header


No Alert = no need to do any further checking
Decision Required = to check further in ComplyAdvantage:

  • Can refer to SOP here SOP for Handling Screening Hits and raise to #compliance-request for clearance

For manual screening if not all directors / shareholders submitted as stakeholder in AD, can follow SOP here Merchant Screening SOP.

Note: If merchant provide Business Shareholding Chart, MO needs to check again in Comply Advantage website for all new shareholders.

9

Once all ok to proceed, to tag Raffy John Panes& Jinghang Gui in the slack thread for them to proceed further checking & approval

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.

  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit

  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit

  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Additionally, merchants operating in Malaysia must adhere to additional standards to ensure legal transparency. Their website/app must include:

  • Terms & Conditions, Privacy Policy, and Refund Policy.

  • Contact information (email, telephone number, address to follow the business address as per SSM)

  • SSM company registered number and registered name to verify ownership

  • Must display the Registered Company Name, Registration Number, and Contact Details ( number, company address and email address) in the profile info.

Please also refrain from onboarding merchants to MY/Regional Market (wanting MY channels) if they're using this website urls:

  • https://www.mexc.fm

  • https://www.bitget.com

  • https://bingx.com/en

  • https://www.htx.com/

  • https://www.coinw.com/

  • https://www.xt.com/en

  • https://primexbt.com/

  • https://www.bitunix.com/

  • https://www.tradeeu.global

  • https://eobroker.com

  • https://propestcapital.ovrtx.online

  • https://corsa-futures.com

  • https://corsafuturestrade.com

  • https://cftrades.com

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

1

Ensure showing No Similar Matches & follow website requirements:


Social media pages - is optional

MO

2

Tick on if the specific document/field is correct and valid.

Tick if the information is incorrect.

3

Ensure not prohibited/high risk & some NOB require further licenses / EDD questionnaire:

Refer to SOP here [Regional Compliance Working Paper] High-Risk Industry Mapping List for further licenses needed. (Tab: industry risk matching)

  • Check whether column B has tick / tick

  • Next, refer to column CW whether tag on license

  • Lastly refer to column CZ on license name (compiling)

Then MO can decide whether to escalate to Compliance Team at Slack Channel #compliance-requests for high risk merchant

4

Additional Requirement is needed for merchants operating as a marketplace.

With this, MO need to ask merchant to complete the EDD questionnaire:
Digital Platform Due Diligence Questionnaire

After merchant filla out and submitted the form, please raise to #compliance-request for clearance:

Sample escalation: https://xendit.slack.com/archives/C08GRB0HD4Z/p1759213365005469

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Diligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not (bank book scan is not needed to be checked anymore not unless needed e.g. High Risk Industry and if requested by Compliance).

2

MO need to check Director & proof of residency

  • For MY NRIC definitely address stated, then no need to request further

  • If the stakeholder upload passport (address not stated), will need the proof of residency


- Business shareholding chart & UBO final layer to show proof of residency
Slack convo: https://xendit.slack.com/archives/C07QKRW5YS1/p1756357905794039

- UBO of top / final layer required for individual shareholder >25% shareholding (standard practice in MY) to share us proof of residency

- If merchant provided passport (which is without address details), will need to provide further documents such as utility bill or tenancy agreement with landlord to show proof of residing in that address

3

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

If required further information / documents / license from merchant, MO needs to cllick on Action > Request Resubmission and to fill up the English part (max 220 characters).

MO

2

Ensure to update in MO notes (without using BDD / LDD) https://xendit.slack.com/archives/C083M8JH35W/p1755163370984989

3

Status will then change to “awaiting resubmission”

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, click on “Action”

2

Can choose “pass” if pass for all KYC & ComplyAdvantage

confirm merchant sign the e-consent:

Fail = if not pass

3

Once all ok to proceed, to tag Raffy John Panes& Jinghang Gui in the slack thread for them to proceed further checking & approval

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS

  • AML

  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant failed KYC, click on “Action”

2

Can choose “failed” if merchant fails to pass the KYC
